info.title: calm-server
info.description: Wire-format contract between calm-server (Rust) and web-calm (TS). Source of truth for generated TypeScript types.

get.summary:
Look up the Terminal row a card owns. Returns 404 if the card has no
terminal (yet). The UI uses this to validate a card_id cached in
localStorage before attempting a WS attach to its terminal.

parameters.name: card_id
parameters.description: Card id (must be a terminal card)

parameters.name: card_id
parameters.description: Card id (must be a terminal card)

requestBody.description: Optional body — empty means use defaults

400.description: Card is not a terminal card

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description:
Optional. When omitted, returns every overlay of `entity_kind`
across the workspace — the sidebar uses this form to render
accurate per-wave status without fetching each wave's detail.

200.description: Overlays for an entity (or all of a kind when entity_id is omitted)
Installed plugins with their runtime state
Catalog of views from currently enabled plugins
Manifest reloaded + plugin restarted if enabled

400.description: Manifest invalid / id mismatch after reload

parameters.name: view_id
parameters.description: View id within the plugin manifest

404.description: Plugin or view not found / asset missing

200.description: Tool result JSON (shape depends on dispatched neige.* callback)

parameters.name: wave_id
parameters.description: Wave id this card belongs to

404.description: Plugin not running / not in registry

kind.description:
`"terminal"` for built-in PTY cards, `"plugin:<plugin-id>:<view-id>"`
for plugin-provided cards. Kernel never interprets beyond that prefix.

CreateCardBody.description:
Body payload accepted by `POST /api/waves/:wave_id/cards`.

Two mutually-exclusive paths:
  * **Direct create** — `kind`, `sort`, `payload`, `title` set (legacy
    pre-M2 wire). The kernel writes the row verbatim.
  * **`via_tool_call`** — kernel invokes the plugin's tool, extracts the
    `ui://` resource URI from `_meta.ui.resourceUri`, persists a Card with
    `kind = <resource_uri>` and `payload = structuredContent`.

When both are sent, `via_tool_call` wins. Documented in this module's
header. We keep the legacy fields alongside via `#[serde(flatten)]` so
existing clients (web-calm AddPanel for terminal/doc cards) keep working
unchanged.

kind.description:
Legacy direct-create fields. Mirrors `NewCard` shape; `wave_id` is
taken from the path so we omit it here.

oneOf.description:
M2: plugin tool-call descriptor. When present, the kernel calls the
plugin and the `kind` / `payload` fields above are ignored.

ErrorBody.description:
JSON shape returned for every error response — `{error, code}`.
Mirrors the body produced by `CalmError::into_response`. Hand-written
duplicate of the in-line `json!` body so OpenAPI consumers see a
concrete schema.

oneOf.description:
Catch-all so we can return a friendly 400 for tarball/url/etc. instead
of a serde deserialize error. Slice D scope is `local_path` only.

wave_id.description:
Defaulted so the REST handler can override from the `:wave_id` path
param without forcing every client body to repeat it. Direct repo
callers must still set this — passing "" produces a NotFound.

sort.description: If absent, server appends to end.

cwd.description: Empty string or missing → `$HOME` (then cwd of server).

env.description: Extra env on top of the inherited set. JSON object: `{"FOO":"bar"}`.

kind.description: Plugin-defined string. Kernel does not interpret.

entity_id.description:
Optional. When omitted, returns every overlay of `entity_kind`
across the workspace — the sidebar uses this form to render
accurate per-wave status without fetching each wave's detail.

PluginDetail.description:
Single-plugin detail returned by GET-by-id, install, enable, disable,
config-patch, and reload. The full manifest blob rides along so the UI can
render version/author/views without a separate fetch.

PluginListItem.description:
Compact row used by `GET /api/plugins`. Pairs the persisted `Plugin` row
with the runtime status the supervisor knows about. The full manifest is
excluded here to keep the list payload cheap; callers needing the manifest
hit `GET /api/plugins/:id`.

state.description:
Wire-name string per design §7.1: `running | spawning | crashed |
disabled | installing | installed`.

ToolCallBody.description:
M5: AppBridge → kernel tool-call wire body. Mirrors the JSON-RPC
`tools/call` params shape so the web-calm helper can hand it through
verbatim from the iframe-side `app.callServerTool({ name, arguments })`.

ViewCatalogEntry.description:
One entry in the `/api/plugins/views` catalog. Used by Slice G's AddPanel.

M3 (mcp-apps migration) adds `resource_uri` as the canonical
`ui://<plugin>/<view>` identifier. `plugin_id` + `view_id` stay on the
wire alongside it during the M3→M4 transition; M4's frontend pivots to
`resource_uri` and a follow-up slice may drop the legacy pair.

resource_uri.description:
Canonical MCP Apps URI: `ui://<plugin_id>/<view_id>`. Always present —
computed kernel-side so the frontend doesn't have to redo the join.

scope.description: `"card"` for M3 — wave/cove are banned per design §10.

WaveDetail.description:
What a Wave detail page renders: the wave itself plus its cards and
any overlays scoped to the wave (status/progress badges) and its cards.

archived_at.description:
Pass `Some(Some(ts))` to archive, `Some(None)` to unarchive,
or omit (`None`) to leave alone.
